questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Match the following Lists.(a $\rightarrow$ edge length, $\mathrm{r}+\rightarrow$ radius of cation, $\mathrm{r} \rightarrow \rightarrow$ radius of anion)
\(\begin{array}{l|l} \text{List-I} & \text{List-II} \\ \hline \text{(P) NaCl type crystal} & \text{(1) } \sqrt{3} \mathrm{a}=4\left(\mathrm{r}_{+}+\mathrm{r}_{-}\right) \\ \text{(Q) ZnS type crystal} & \text{(2) } \sqrt{3} a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right) \\ \text{(R) CsCl type crystal} & \text{(3) } \sqrt{2} a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right) \\ \begin{array}{l} \text{(S) } \mathrm{A}_3 \mathrm{B} \text{ type crystal in which anion B} \\ \text{forms simple cubic unit cell and cation A} \\ \text{occupies all the square planar voids} \end{array} & \text{(4) } a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right) \end{array}\)
questions.solution:
In NaCl crystal Cl ions form fcc unit cell and $\mathrm{Na}^{+}$ ions occupy all theoctahedral voids.
$\therefore \quad a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right)$
In ZnS crystal $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ ions form fcc unit cell and $\mathrm{Zn}^{2+}$ ions occupy alternate tetrahedral voids
$\therefore \quad \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a=\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right)$
In CsCl crystal, $\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$ions form simple cubic unit cell and $\mathrm{Cs}^{+}$ions occupy the centre of each cube
$\therefore \quad \sqrt{3} a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right)$
In $\mathrm{A}_3 \mathrm{~B}$ crystal, anions $\mathrm{B}^{-3}$ ions form simple cubic unit cell and cations $\mathrm{A}^{+}$occupy square planar voids.
$\therefore \quad \sqrt{2} a=2\left(r_{+}+r_{-}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
